Next the author gives his own opinion:]
What difference does it make whether there is a single Ketu
. or many ? Under any circumstance, its effects ought to be declared

124 Bfhat Samhitu
through the positions of its appearance and setting, contact with
planets or asterisms, smoky mantle and colours.
[Here the author shows himself to be a practical thinker,
not a mere theorist. What are required for our practical purpose are
the particular part of the sky in which a Ketu appears or disappears
and the effects that accrue to the people. Similarly one ought to
observe their colours, smoky nature and contact with planets or
stars. For, each one of these circumstances'is supposed to yield
a particular result.]
